scope: Adopted items only. This ledger tracks the elevenlabs-ui components swiftcn has chosen to port; it is not an inventory of the full upstream catalog.

structuralMaps.name: live-waveform
behaviors: live bars from sampled levels while active, throttled to the update rate
static mode mirrors low-to-high bands symmetrically around the center
scrolling mode pushes an averaged level into a capped history anchored at the trailing edge
processing plays a three-wave synthesized animation that blends out of the last live frame
fade-to-idle shrinks remaining bars away after activity ends
dotted resting baseline while neither active nor processing
bar width, minimum height, gap, radius, color, and sensitivity configuration
edge fading with configurable fade width capped at 30 percent per side
bar opacity scales 0.4-1.0 with level; bar height is 80 percent of the container times level
accessibility label switches between live, processing, and idle descriptions
intentionalAdaptations: SCAudioLevelProvider polling replaces the embedded getUserMedia/AnalyserNode capture; deviceId, fftSize, smoothingTimeConstant, onError, onStreamReady, and onStreamEnd are engine concerns and move to the provider
TimelineView(.animation) plus Canvas replace the requestAnimationFrame canvas loop; upstream's per-frame step constants are integrated per second so motion is refresh-rate independent
theme.foreground replaces the CSS currentColor default for barColor
height is a fixed point value with nil filling the proposal, replacing CSS string heights
the destination-out edge gradient is reproduced with GraphicsContext blend modes

structuralMaps.name: bar-visualizer
behaviors: connecting, initializing, listening, speaking, and thinking agent states
connecting sweeps a mirrored highlight pair across all bars in two seconds; initializing steps the same sweep at one second per frame
listening blinks the center bar at 500 ms; thinking blinks it at 150 ms with a 300 ms pulse
speaking and stateless render all bars in the primary color
demo mode generates sine-plus-noise bands at 50 ms while speaking or listening and rests at 0.2 otherwise
bar height clamps between minHeight and maxHeight percent with the +5 offset
bars are flexible 8-12 pt capsules with 6 pt gaps and 150 ms transitions
bottom alignment by default with an opt-in center alignment
muted rounded container with 16 pt padding
level changes below 0.01 are ignored to avoid churn
intentionalAdaptations: SCAudioLevelProvider polling replaces the mediaStream prop and the Web Audio useAudioVolume/useMultibandVolume hooks; loPass/hiPass band splitting and analyser configuration are engine concerns
the useBarAnimator requestAnimationFrame sequencer becomes a pure function of elapsed TimelineView time with the same sequences and intervals
deterministic hash noise replaces Math.random in demo mode so frames are reproducible
a height parameter replaces upstream's h-32 class and className height overrides
Capsule shapes and theme tokens replace Tailwind utilities; animate-pulse at 300 ms becomes a time-driven opacity cycle

structuralMaps.name: speech-input
behaviors: idle microphone button expands into a recording bar with cancel, preview, and stop controls
connecting shows a pulsing primary dot and disables the record button; recording shows a destructive stop square at 80 percent scale
the preview strip reveals to 112 pt with trailing-pinned text, 10 pt edge fades, an italic placeholder, and a tooltip of the full text
cancel collapses to zero width and is inert until connected
transcript building joins committed segments and the partial transcript exactly as buildTranscript
onChange fires on transcript changes; onStart, onStop, onCancel, and onError fire with transcript snapshots
start resets transcripts and guards against superseded starts, disconnecting a stale connection
cancel hands the discarded snapshot to onCancel after clearing
the session disconnects when the component disappears
default, sm, and lg size variants map to 36/32/40 pt square controls
recording chrome (background, 1 pt inset border, hairline shadow) appears only while connected
accessibility labels for start/stop and cancel; the hidden preview and cancel are removed from accessibility
intentionalAdaptations: the SCSpeechInputSession protocol replaces useScribe plus getToken: it mirrors the hook surface the component consumes (status, partialTranscript, committedTranscripts, error, connect/disconnect/clearTranscripts) while token acquisition, model/VAD/microphone configuration, and the Scribe error taxonomy (onAuthError, onQuotaExceededError, ...) live in the conforming engine; no ElevenLabs SDK is required
committedTranscripts surfaces the segment texts the component consumes, not upstream's TranscriptSegment records
SCSpeechInputContext in the SwiftUI environment replaces the React context and the useSpeechInput hook
SCSkeleton renders the upstream circular Skeleton connecting indicator with the shared reduced-motion-aware pulse engine
SF Symbols replace the lucide MicIcon/SquareIcon/XIcon; animation modifiers replace framer-motion and the CSS width/opacity transitions
the recording chrome uses one theme.background fill where upstream additionally swaps to bg-muted in dark mode
onChange is suppressed for the reset performed by start/cancel, matching upstream's event-driven onChange

structuralMaps.name: response
behaviors: renders a markdown string: headings, paragraphs, lists, blockquotes, fenced code, tables, links, inline code, and thematic breaks
no top margin above the first block and no bottom margin below the last, upstream's first/last-child margin trimming
the body is skipped when an equal markdown string is re-created, upstream's memo children comparator
fills the available width with leading alignment
intentionalAdaptations: MarkdownUI (gonzalezreal/swift-markdown-ui, 2.4.1) replaces Streamdown as the wrapped engine because Streamdown is JS-only; the registry item declares it in its dependencies field exactly as upstream declares the streamdown npm package, the library's first and only SPM dependency

markdown styles map to theme tokens through MarkdownUI's theming API: text and headings on theme.foreground, inline code and code-block surfaces on theme.muted, table lines, the blockquote rule, code-block borders, and horizontal rules on theme.border, links on theme.primary, h6 on theme.mutedForeground; font sizes are relative so the response inherits the caller's font
Equatable conformance keyed on the markdown string replaces React.memo
upstream's size-full becomes a leading full-width frame with natural height so the view composes inside ScrollView and chat rows

known gap, watched: MarkdownUI has no streaming-specific rendering of incomplete markdown — an unterminated code fence or emphasis renders literally until its closing delimiter streams in, where Streamdown renders it as already complete

structuralMaps.name: scrub-bar
behaviors: compound container publishes duration, value, progress, and scrub lifecycle callbacks to every part
track converts press and drag position into a duration-scaled time clamped to the track bounds
scrub start fires once on press, scrub fires throughout movement, and scrub end fires on release
8 point secondary track contains an immediate primary progress fill and a 16 point primary thumb centered on progress
progress and thumb clamp out-of-range values visually to 0-100 percent
time labels use tabular m:ss formatting and coerce invalid or negative values to 0:00
track exposes slider value and bounds to assistive technology
intentionalAdaptations: SCScrubBarContext in the SwiftUI environment replaces React context and useScrubBarContext; parts render nothing outside a container instead of throwing
DragGesture(minimumDistance: 0) replaces pointer capture on the browser window
progress is represented as a 0-1 fraction rather than a CSS percentage
SCScrubBarProgress composes SCProgressTrack and SCProgressIndicator; a zero animation duration reproduces upstream's transition-none scrub override
a native accessibility adjustable action seeks in five-percent increments

structuralMaps.name: transcript-viewer
behaviors: character alignment composes into timed words and whitespace gaps while optionally removing bracketed audio tags
current word follows the playback clock with binary-search lookup, initial first-word highlighting, and timing-gap snapping
context exposes original alignment, all segments and words, spoken/current/unspoken projections, playback state, time, duration, seeking, and scrubbing commands
context exposes seek-to-word by value and index for custom renderers; the default word remains noninteractive like upstream's span
words flow inline with spoken foreground, muted unspoken, and primary current-word styling; the complete transcript becomes spoken at the end
custom word and gap renderers receive the same segment status as the default renderer
play-pause control supports a custom label and announces its current action
context-aware scrub bar binds the shared clock and shows elapsed and remaining time
play, pause, time-update, ended, and duration-change callbacks follow player state changes
player duration wins when known, otherwise the final alignment or word end time supplies a best-effort duration
intentionalAdaptations: SCTranscriptViewerPlayer replaces the HTMLAudioElement and TranscriptViewerAudio mount point; the engine owns source loading, media type, volume, and playback
Observation of the injected player replaces requestAnimationFrame and DOM media event listeners
SCTranscriptViewerContext in the SwiftUI environment plus SCTranscriptViewerProvider replace React context/provider and useTranscriptViewerContext
an additional composition initializer accepts normalized word timing directly when an ASR provider does not expose ElevenLabs character alignment; alignment is nil in that context
a custom SwiftUI Layout replaces inline spans while preserving word wrapping and hanging whitespace gaps
SwiftUI view-builder slots replace renderWord/renderGap and class-name hooks; SF Symbols replace lucide icons
parts render nothing outside a provider instead of throwing

structuralMaps.name: waveform
behaviors: static waveform resamples normalized amplitude data into the bars that fit and maps bar clicks back to source indices
all renderers share minimum-height centered bars, 0.3-1.0 level opacity, configurable geometry and color, and a two-sided edge fade capped at 20 percent
static placeholder waveform generates 0.2-0.8 amplitudes while the scrolling waveform continuously spawns and advances bars
audio scrubber overlays progress tint, a two-point playhead, and an optional 16 point handle, and converts press/drag position into seek time
microphone waveform mirrors sampled low bands while active, blends into a three-wave processing animation, and fades to idle
live microphone waveform captures a capped trailing history, becomes pannable after capture, optionally plays scrub snippets, and follows resumed playback
recording waveform grows at the trailing edge, snapshots the completed take, and exposes a draggable position indicator
scrubbable variants expose slider state and native adjustable actions to assistive technology
intentionalAdaptations: TimelineView plus Canvas replace requestAnimationFrame canvas loops and make animation rates refresh-rate independent
deterministic seeded noise replaces Math.random so placeholder and generated frames are reproducible
an empty scrolling data collection selects synthesized heights, replacing the upstream optional collection boundary
SCAudioLevelProvider replaces getUserMedia and AnalyserNode capture; microphone selection, FFT sizing, smoothing, and errors live in the engine
SCWaveformRecordingPlayback replaces MediaRecorder, AudioBuffer decoding, playback rate, and low-pass scrub-audio glue
DragGesture replaces document mouse capture; SwiftUI Bindings replace saved history and drag-offset refs
the playback-follow calculation pans from recording start to live edge, correcting the upstream path's mixed drag sign convention
theme foreground and point-valued height replace CSS variables and CSS string heights
native accessibility adjustable actions augment upstream slider semantics

structuralMaps.name: audio-level-provider
upstreamParts: AnalyserNode binding embedded in live-waveform and bar-visualizer
behaviors: vends the current spectrum resampled to a requested band count, low to high, normalized 0-1
polled on the main actor by the visualizer views at the upstream update rates
intentionalAdaptations: a shared protocol seam replaces the Web Audio glue duplicated inside both upstream files, following upstream's own hooks-as-registry-items distribution model (use-scribe)
